PHASE: Phase 7 #15 (legacy-socket-retirement) — COMPLETE. Squash-merged to main 2026-05-19 as `af43276`. Test suite 144 → 160 (+16: 8 server handler + 5 client handler + 3 router initiate). Closes `[[parallel-socket-servers-coexistence]]` from Phase 7 #11 — auth-gated server is now the single source of truth for socket events. First REAL end-to-end implementation of incoming-call — prior production state had the dialog connecting to a 503 placeholder.

LESSONS_ADDED (0 this session — Phase 7 #15 implementation):
  Nothing novel emerged. Existing patterns covered everything: [[pure-helper-extraction-pattern]] for client handler, [[socket-revalidation-test-pattern]] for server fakes, [[event-handler-disposer-test-pattern]] for client handler tests, [[instrumentation-edge-stub-required]] for build gate (calls.ts joins createSocketServer chain), [[nodemailer-cve-mitigation]] for audit threshold. New process observation worth noting (but not encoded as typed lesson yet because it's already implicit in [[sonnet-thrash-sessionstart-hooks]] + memory-governance.md §1 Step 2.5 30K budget gate): Sonnet subagents handle single-file TDD tasks well but thrash on (a) multi-file integration touching 3+ files in one dispatch, (b) governance-doc edits spanning 1000+ lines per file. Future tickets with similar shape should either pre-bake context aggressively (one bash with all relevant excerpts in one tool call) or stay inline. The Task 5 + Task 12 thrash recoveries in this ticket are evidence of the 30K gate working as designed: detect → re-decompose → controller fallback.

TIER_CLASSIFICATION: 2 — moderate. Per memory-governance §1: 12 file touches across 4 modules with dependency depth 3 (types → server-handler → server-factory → router → component). Final score = (12 × 2.5) + (4 × 5) + (3 × 3) = 30 + 20 + 9 = 59 — slightly below design-phase estimate of 61.5 (because plan and one test file were collapsed). Below Tier 3 threshold of 80, within 80K Opus 4.7 SAFE zone for single-session inline execution. Actual execution: subagent-driven with 2 Sonnet thrash recoveries handled by Opus controller inline. Single inline Opus 4.7 session would have been comparable in wall time (estimated ~70K controller context) — the subagent path traded controller efficiency for reviewer independence + clearer per-task audit trail before squash.
